title: Repository Guidelines
text: R/ organized durable workflow domains. Current modules include preprocessing (preprocessing.R), clustering/integration (analysis_clustering.R), differential expression (analysis_de.R), enrichment (analysis_enrichment.R), metrics/composition (analysis_metrics.R), example data (data_examples.R), IO (data_io.R), interpretation (interpretation.R), package helpers (package_tools.R), signatures (signatures.R), visualization (visualization.R), small utilities (utils.R). man/ contains roxygen2-generated .Rd files. Treat generated output keep synchronized roxygen comments R/. tests/testthat/ currently small unit-test surface; add focused tests near behavior change. vignettes/ contains longer workflows. Keep chunks check-safe avoid unconditional network access heavyweight setup examples. data/ stores package datasets. docs/codex/ stores package-maintainer Codex docs modernization memory; already excluded package builds via .Rbuildignore. inst/codex/project-template/ stores shipped initialized-project governance template. inst/codex/package-skills/ stores shipped package-usage Codex skills. Keep repository-planning modernization memory installed user assets. _pkgdown.yml .github/workflows/ define package website CI entry points.

title: Repository Guidelines
text: Rscript -e 'testthat::test_local(stop_on_failure = TRUE)' runs local test suite. Rscript -e 'testthat::test_local(filter = "composition", stop_on_failure = TRUE)' runs current focused tests iterating composition-related changes. Rscript -e '(requireNamespace("devtools", quietly = TRUE)) devtools::document() else stop("devtools installed")' regenerates NAMESPACE man/ roxygen changes. R CMD build . builds package tarball. R CMD check ---manual Shennong_*.tar.gz preferred package check successful build.

title: Repository Guidelines
text: Preserve public behavior unless change explicitly justified, documented, validated. Prefer small, reviewable refactors repo-wide rewrites. Keep exported user-facing functions strict sn_verb_noun naming family. introduce camelCase, dot.case, mixed prefixes. Reuse existing sn_* naming families whenever possible. Keep internal helpers unexported clearly named. Prefer explicit namespace usage roxygen @importFrom entries hidden dependencies. Keep examples deterministic safe package checks; wrap network- dependency-heavy examples \dontrun{} make conditional. Avoid introducing new package dependencies unless remove concrete maintenance problem solved simply.

title: Repository Guidelines
text: Add update tests changing behavior risky areas. Prefer lightweight tests require external downloads optional heavyweight packages unless function contract truly depends . Run narrowest relevant tests first, rerun full local suite closing milestone. roxygen, exports, package metadata change, regenerate documentation rerun relevant validation commands. add change user-facing function, parameter, stored-result schema, workflow, must also update relevant pkgdown article(s) shipped Codex assets inst/codex/project-template/ inst/codex/package-skills/ change set affected. user-facing change set must also update NEWS.md release notes reflect shipped behavior. New stored-result retrieval interpretation features must documented user level least one concrete example showing discover stored result retrieve Seurat object. updating user-facing feature, rebuild pkgdown locally rendered site matches current package sources. Updating vignette/reference source files sufficient.

title: Repository Guidelines
text: Keep modernization step self-contained: one small change set, one validation pass, one update docs/codex/Status.md docs/codex/Decisions.md. overwrite unrelated working tree changes; repository may dirty. Record compatibility notes, validation commands, rationale non-obvious changes docs/codex/. Breaking changes require explicit documentation docs/codex/Decisions.md NEWS.md. Use Conventional Commits commit messages. Preferred commit format: <type>(<scope>): <summary>. Typical types repo: feat, fix, refactor, docs, test, build, ci, chore. Keep summary imperative specific, example: feat(clustering): consolidate quick integration workflows. change breaking, add ! type scope explain break commit body NEWS.md.

title: Repository Guidelines
text: Likely public API surface: IO (sn_read, sn_write, dataset loading), Seurat initialization/normalization/QC, clustering/integration/annotation, plotting, signatures/enrichment, composition/metrics. Current high-risk zones data import/export, heavyweight optional-package integrations, schema stored object@misc. Current modernization order : inventory API, stabilize tests, normalize metadata namespace handling, reorganize source files without changing behavior, tighten internals documentation. Repository-internal developer memory stays docs/codex/ AGENTS.md. Installed-package user assets belong inst/codex/project-template/ inst/codex/package-skills/.

Use --help see available --skip-* options.

title: Markers, signatures, pathways, and annotation
text: Marker tables useful want name clusters manually. trusted reference already exists, sn_transfer_labels() follows Seurat’s anchor workflow writes projected label plus confidence score back query metadata. wrapper keeps source label transfer settings query@misc$label_transfer, annotation just loose metadata column. query-first interface can use Coralysis reference mapping reference trained sn_run_cluster(integration_method = "coralysis"). mapping, keep Coralysis SingleCellExperiment PCA model reference leaving store_sce = TRUE return.model = TRUE.
code: reference <- pbmc query <- pbmc  reference$cell_type <- reference$seurat_clusters  query <- sn_transfer_labels(   object = query,   reference = reference,   label_by = "cell_type",   prediction_prefix = "pbmc_reference",   dims = 1:15,   verbose = FALSE )  table(query$pbmc_reference_label) head(query[[]][, c("pbmc_reference_label", "pbmc_reference_score")]) reference <- sn_run_cluster(   reference,   batch = "sample_id",   integration_method = "coralysis",   normalization_method = "seurat",   integration_control = list(     store_sce = TRUE,     pca_args = list(return.model = TRUE)   ),   verbose = FALSE )  query <- sn_transfer_labels(   object = query,   reference = reference,   label_by = "cell_type",   method = "coralysis",   prediction_prefix = "coral_reference",   transfer_control = list(k.nn = 10),   verbose = FALSE )

title: Markers, signatures, pathways, and annotation
text: Cell communication run real backend rather ad hoc ligand-receptor table. sn_run_cell_communication() keeps stored-result pattern used DE enrichment. Use CellChat global interaction network, NicheNet question sender--receiver ligand activity, LIANA optional LIANA package available consensus scoring preferred. NicheNet needs ligand-target matrix ligand-receptor network. Shennong requires priors explicitly output remains traceable real NicheNet model rather inferred placeholder network. transcription-factor pathway activity, sn_run_regulatory_activity() uses fast footprint methods decoupleR. DoRothEA reports TF activity; PROGENy reports pathway activity.
code: pbmc <- sn_run_cell_communication(   object = pbmc,   method = "cellchat",   group_by = "seurat_clusters",   species = "human",   store_name = "cluster_cellchat" )  cellchat_tbl <- sn_get_cell_communication_result(   pbmc,   communication_name = "cluster_cellchat" )  head(cellchat_tbl) pbmc <- sn_run_cell_communication(   object = pbmc,   method = "nichenetr",   group_by = "seurat_clusters",   sender = c("0", "1"),   receiver = "2",   geneset = c("IL7R", "CCR7", "LTB"),   ligand_target_matrix = ligand_target_matrix,   lr_network = lr_network,   store_name = "sender_receiver_nichenet" ) pbmc <- sn_run_regulatory_activity(   object = pbmc,   method = "dorothea",   group_by = "seurat_clusters",   species = "human",   store_name = "cluster_dorothea" )  tf_activity <- sn_get_regulatory_activity_result(   pbmc,   activity_name = "cluster_dorothea",   sources = c("NFKB1", "STAT1") )  head(tf_activity)

what: Build a small reference and mock bulk matrix
title: Bulk deconvolution from a PBMC3k reference
text: mock bulk samples sums selected single-cell clusters. Real bulk data normalized gene-aligned according backend requirements.
code: library(Shennong) library(Seurat) library(dplyr)  pbmc <- sn_load_data("pbmc3k") #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:17:17] Initializing Seurat object for project: pbmc3k. #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:17:18] Running QC metrics for human. #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:17:18] Seurat object initialization complete.  pbmc <- sn_run_cluster(   object = pbmc,   normalization_method = "seurat",   nfeatures = 1500,   dims = 1:15,   resolution = 0.6,   species = "human",   verbose = FALSE )  pbmc$cell_type <- paste0("cluster_", pbmc$seurat_clusters)  counts <- SeuratObject::LayerData(pbmc, assay = "RNA", layer = "counts")  bulk <- cbind(   bulk_a = Matrix::rowSums(counts[, pbmc$cell_type %in% c("cluster_0", "cluster_1"), drop = FALSE]),   bulk_b = Matrix::rowSums(counts[, pbmc$cell_type %in% c("cluster_2", "cluster_3"), drop = FALSE]) )

what: Prepare a CIBERSORTx run without launching the container
title: Bulk deconvolution from a PBMC3k reference
text: Use cibersortx_dry_run = TRUE validate exported files command bundle locally. right mode examples, tests, documentation. actual CIBERSORTx run, store credentials locally omit placeholder values.
code: bundle <- sn_deconvolve_bulk(   x = pbmc,   bulk = bulk,   method = "cibersortx",   cell_type_by = "cell_type",   outdir = file.path(tempdir(), "pbmc3k-cibersortx"),   prefix = "pbmc3k_demo",   cibersortx_email = "demo@example.org",   cibersortx_token = "fake-token",   cibersortx_dry_run = TRUE,   return_object = FALSE )  names(bundle) #> [1] "table"     "files"     "artifacts" "method" bundle$command #> NULL bundle$files #> $single_cell_reference #> [1] "/tmp/Rtmpr69Pra/pbmc3k-cibersortx/sample_file_for_cibersort.txt" #>  #> $mixture #> [1] "/tmp/Rtmpr69Pra/pbmc3k-cibersortx/mixture_file_for_cibersort.txt" #>  #> $signature_matrix #> [1] "/tmp/Rtmpr69Pra/pbmc3k-cibersortx/CIBERSORTx_sample_file_for_cibersort_inferred_phenoclasses.CIBERSORTx_sample_file_for_cibersort_inferred_refsample.bm.K999.txt" #>  #> $result #> [1] "/tmp/Rtmpr69Pra/pbmc3k-cibersortx/CIBERSORTx_pbmc3k_demo_Results.txt" sn_set_cibersortx_credentials(   email = Sys.getenv("CIBERSORTX_EMAIL"),   token = Sys.getenv("CIBERSORTX_TOKEN") )  result <- sn_deconvolve_bulk(   x = pbmc,   bulk = bulk,   method = "cibersortx",   cell_type_by = "cell_type",   outdir = "results/deconvolution/cibersortx",   prefix = "pbmc3k" )

title: Bulk deconvolution from a PBMC3k reference
text: CIBERSORTx already produced fraction table, import cibersortx_result store table explicitly. Keeping results reference object makes downstream retrieval predictable.
code: fractions <- data.frame(   sample = c("bulk_a", "bulk_b"),   cluster_0 = c(0.45, 0.10),   cluster_1 = c(0.35, 0.05),   cluster_2 = c(0.10, 0.50),   cluster_3 = c(0.10, 0.35),   check.names = FALSE )  pbmc <- sn_store_deconvolution(   object = pbmc,   result = fractions,   store_name = "pbmc3k_mock_bulk",   method = "cibersortx",   bulk_samples = fractions$sample,   reference_label = "cell_type",   artifacts = bundle$files,   return_object = TRUE )  sn_get_deconvolution_result(   pbmc,   deconvolution_name = "pbmc3k_mock_bulk" ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   sample cluster_0 cluster_1 cluster_2 cluster_3 #>   <chr>      <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 bulk_a      0.45      0.35       0.1      0.1  #> 2 bulk_b      0.1       0.05       0.5      0.35

what: One function for the standard clustering path
title: Clustering and integration with PBMC3k
text: minimal clustering call intentionally short. arguments usually matter report visible: normalization method, number features, PC range, resolution, clustering algorithm, species. input already contains sn_run_cluster() stage metadata, function reuses matching stages default. makes resolution tuning cheap: sn_run_cluster(pbmc, resolution = 1.0) reuses normalization, HVGs, PCA, neighbor graph, UMAP, recomputes cluster labels. Use rerun_from = "integration" changing integration backend, reuse = FALSE completely fresh run needed. result regular Seurat object. can use Shennong plotting helpers drop Seurat whenever needed.
code: library(Shennong) library(Seurat) library(dplyr)  pbmc <- sn_load_data("pbmc3k") #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:19:00] Initializing Seurat object for project: pbmc3k. #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:19:00] Running QC metrics for human. #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:19:01] Seurat object initialization complete.  pbmc <- sn_run_cluster(   object = pbmc,   normalization_method = "seurat",   nfeatures = 2000,   npcs = 30,   dims = 1:20,   resolution = 0.6,   cluster_algorithm = "leiden",   species = "human",   verbose = FALSE )  pbmc #> An object of class Seurat  #> 54872 features across 2753 samples within 1 assay  #> Active assay: RNA (54872 features, 2000 variable features) #>  3 layers present: counts, data, scale.data #>  2 dimensional reductions calculated: pca, umap sn_plot_dim(   object = pbmc,   reduction = "umap",   group_by = "seurat_clusters",   label = TRUE,   title = "PBMC3k clusters" )

what: Integration is the same API with a batch column
title: Clustering and integration with PBMC3k
text: PBMC3k one dataset, example creates teaching-pseudo batch column. real experiment, batch donor, library, chemistry, dataset column. keeps mental model small: single-sample clustering batch integration use entry point; adding batch turns integration, integration_method names backend. title: Composition and differential abundance
text: Global proportions can miss local shifts inside embedding. sn_run_milo() wraps miloR neighborhood workflow sample-level differential abundance. milo result generated elsewhere, store retrieve object-level convention used DE enrichment. sn_plot_milo() displays stored neighborhood-level table.  recommended reporting pattern show global composition local neighborhood abundance biological claim depends changes cell-state frequency.
code: milo_tbl <- sn_run_milo(   x = pbmc,   sample_by = "sample",   group_by = "condition",   contrast = c("stim", "control"),   reduction = "pca",   dims = 1:15,   annotation_by = "cluster_label",   max_cells = 1500,   store_name = "condition_da",   return_object = FALSE )  head(milo_tbl) mock_milo <- data.frame(   logFC = c(1.1, -0.7, 0.2),   SpatialFDR = c(0.03, 0.08, 0.4),   cluster_label = c("cluster_0", "cluster_1", "cluster_2") )  pbmc <- sn_store_milo(   object = pbmc,   result = mock_milo,   store_name = "condition_da",   sample_by = "sample",   group_by = "condition",   comparison = "stim_vs_control",   reduction = "pca",   annotation_by = "cluster_label",   return_object = TRUE )  sn_get_milo_result(pbmc, milo_name = "condition_da") #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   logFC SpatialFDR cluster_label #>   <dbl>      <dbl> <chr>         #> 1   1.1       0.03 cluster_0     #> 2  -0.7       0.08 cluster_1     #> 3   0.2       0.4  cluster_2 sn_plot_milo(   pbmc,   milo_name = "condition_da",   annotation_by = "cluster_label",   title = "Neighborhood differential abundance" )

text: PCR batch scoring measures much variation embedding explained batch. title: Preprocessing and QC with PBMC3k
text: sn_assess_qc() summarizes object filtering can compare pre-filter object. Store result want later reports recover QC decision without rerunning preprocessing. object now ready clustering. important part just cells genes filtered, rationale summary stored place downstream code can find.
code: qc_report <- sn_assess_qc(   object = pbmc_norm,   reference = pbmc,   sample_by = "sample",   verbose = FALSE )  qc_report$overall #>   n_samples n_cells qc_score qc_label retention_fraction #> 1         1    2597 84.92677     good          0.9433345 #>   low_quality_removed_fraction doublet_removed_fraction #> 1                          NaN                      NaN  pbmc_norm <- sn_assess_qc(   object = pbmc_norm,   reference = pbmc,   sample_by = "sample",   store_name = "pbmc3k_preprocessing",   return_object = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE )  names(pbmc_norm@misc$qc_assessments) #> [1] "pbmc3k_preprocessing"

text: question known marker panel, heatmap usually clearer another embedding. sn_plot_heatmap() validates requested genes can draw either cell-level group-averaged heatmaps. default mode = "cells" follows Seurat’s heatmap backend hides cell names ticks, uses 8 pt group label, rasterizes default, colors group bar Paired palette.  Use mode = "average" comparison group-level pattern rather every individual cell. averages expression within group z-scores gene default, giving compact marker-panel summary. title: Visualization with Shennong
text: Shennong wraps common summary plots report can move cell-level maps sample-level summaries without switching visual grammar.
code: pbmc$sample <- rep(paste0("donor", 1:6), length.out = ncol(pbmc)) pbmc$condition <- ifelse(pbmc$sample %in% paste0("donor", 1:3), "control", "stim")  sn_plot_violin(   object = pbmc,   features = c("nFeature_RNA", "percent.mt"),   group_by = "condition",   palette = "Set2" ) qc_summary <- pbmc[[]] |>   dplyr::group_by(sample, condition) |>   dplyr::summarise(     median_features = median(nFeature_RNA),     median_mito = median(percent.mt),     .groups = "drop"   )  sn_plot_boxplot(   qc_summary,   x = condition,   y = median_features,   y_label = "Median detected genes",   panel_widths = 80,   panel_heights = 70 )

title: Assess integration quality across multiple metrics — sn_assess_integration
code: sn_assess_integration(   x,   batch_by = NULL,   label_by = NULL,   cluster_by = NULL,   reduction = .sn_default_metric_reduction(x),   baseline = NULL,   baseline_reduction = .sn_default_baseline_reduction(x, reduction),   graph = NULL,   dims = NULL,   k = 20,   metrics = NULL,   neighbor_method = c("auto", "graph", "annoy", "exact"),   max_cells = 5000,   stratify_by = NULL,   rare_fraction = 0.02,   rare_n = 50,   challenge_threshold = 0.5,   seed = 717,   n_trees = 50,   batch = NULL,   label = NULL,   cluster = NULL )

title: Assess integration quality across multiple metrics — sn_assess_integration
text: x Seurat object. batch_by Metadata column containing batch labels. label_by Optional metadata column containing biological labels cell type annotations. cluster_by Optional metadata column containing current clustering. reduction Reduction name used primary integrated embedding. Defaults "harmony" present, otherwise "pca". baseline Optional Seurat object used baseline reference PCR batch_by scoring. NULL, baseline taken x. baseline_reduction Optional reduction name used baseline. graph Optional graph name stored x@graphs. dims Optional integer vector embedding dimensions retain. k Number neighbors used graph must built embeddings. metrics Optional character vector selecting metrics compute. Supported values "batch_lisi", "label_lisi", "batch_silhouette", "label_silhouette", "graph_connectivity", "clustering_agreement", "isolated_label_score", "cluster_purity", "cluster_entropy", "pcr_batch", "challenging_groups". neighbor_method Strategy used graph must built. One "auto", "graph", "annoy", "exact". max_cells Optional integer cap used subsample cells running metrics. stratify_by Optional metadata column used preserve representation subsampling. Defaults batch. rare_fraction Fraction--cells threshold flagging rare groups. rare_n Absolute cell-count threshold flagging rare groups. challenge_threshold Threshold derived challenge_score used flag difficult groups. seed Random seed used max_cells triggers subsampling. n_trees Number Annoy trees neighbor_method = "annoy". batch Deprecated alias batch_by. label Deprecated alias label_by. cluster Deprecated alias cluster_by.

title: Assess integration quality across multiple metrics — sn_assess_integration
text: list four top-level elements: summary: one row per summary metric, plus aggregate scores. per_cell: per-cell outputs LISI silhouette. per_group: per-group outputs connectivity, composition, difficult-group diagnostics. parameters: effective runtime configuration.

title: Run differential expression analysis on a Seurat object — sn_find_de
code: if (requireNamespace("Seurat", quietly = TRUE)) {   counts <- matrix(rpois(20 * 24, lambda = 1), nrow = 20, ncol = 24)   rownames(counts) <- c(     paste0("GENE", 1:14),     "CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC", "MS4A1", "CD79A", "HLA-DRA"   )   colnames(counts) <- paste0("cell", 1:24)   counts[c("CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC"), 1:12] <-     counts[c("CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC"), 1:12] + 20   counts[c("MS4A1", "CD79A", "HLA-DRA"), 13:24] <-     counts[c("MS4A1", "CD79A", "HLA-DRA"), 13:24] + 20    obj <- sn_initialize_seurat_object(counts, species = "human")   obj$cell_type <- rep(c("Tcell", "Bcell"), each = 12)   Seurat::Idents(obj) <- obj$cell_type   obj <- Seurat::NormalizeData(obj, verbose = FALSE)    marker_tbl <- sn_find_de(     obj,     analysis = "markers",     group_by = "cell_type",     layer = "data",     min_pct = 0,     logfc_threshold = 0,     return_object = FALSE,     verbose = FALSE   )   head(marker_tbl)    obj <- sn_find_de(     obj,     analysis = "markers",     group_by = "cell_type",     layer = "data",     min_pct = 0,     logfc_threshold = 0,     store_name = "celltype_markers",     return_object = TRUE,     verbose = FALSE   )   names(obj@misc$de_results) } #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:14:44] Initializing Seurat object for project: Shennong. #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:14:44] Running QC metrics for human. #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:14:44] Seurat object initialization complete. #> For a (much!) faster implementation of the Wilcoxon Rank Sum Test, #> (default method for FindMarkers) please install the presto package #> -------------------------------------------- #> install.packages('devtools') #> devtools::install_github('immunogenomics/presto') #> -------------------------------------------- #> After installation of presto, Seurat will automatically use the more  #> efficient implementation (no further action necessary). #> This message will be shown once per session #> [1] "celltype_markers"

title: Interpret cluster markers for cell-type annotation — sn_interpret_annotation
text: object Seurat object. de_name Optional stored marker-result name. omitted, Shennong prefers "default", single available result, otherwise recent marker result. cluster_by Metadata column containing cluster labels. n_markers Number top markers per cluster. marker_selection choose marker genes annotation evidence: "specific" prefers genes relatively unique one cluster, "top" keeps raw top-ranked genes. enrichment_name Optional stored enrichment result used add cluster-level functional evidence. n_terms Number enrichment terms per cluster enrichment_name supplied. enrichment_selection choose pathway/function terms annotation evidence: "specific" prefers terms concentrated fewer clusters, "top" keeps raw top-ranked terms. include_qc Logical; whether include cluster-level QC summaries evidence bundle. reduction Optional dimensional reduction name used summarize cluster neighborhood geometry, example "umap". Use NULL disable geometry evidence. n_neighbor_clusters Number nearest clusters report reduction centroid distances. background Optional study-specific background information provide additional interpretation context. annotation_mode Annotation workflow mode. "single_pass" sends one compact prompt. "agentic" runs broad-pass annotation followed focused refinement pass ambiguous ILC-relevant clusters, reconciles result canonical lineage hints. output_format One "llm" model-ready prompt bundle "human" human-readable summary. provider Optional model provider function. left NULL, Shennong try construct ellmer-backed provider OPENAI_API_KEY plus optional OPENAI_BASE_URL OPENAI_MODEL environment variables. model Optional model identifier. reasoning_effort Optional reasoning effort forwarded compatible GPT-5 chat-completions providers, example "minimal", "low", "medium", "high", "xhigh" upstream endpoint supports . include_json_schema Logical; whether request structured JSON output provider. Defaults TRUE annotation workflows. apply_metadata Logical; TRUE structured annotation response returned, map cluster labels back onto cell Seurat metadata. metadata_prefix Prefix used metadata columns written back Seurat object apply_metadata = TRUE. metadata_fields Annotation fields write back Seurat metadata apply_metadata = TRUE. Defaults core fields : primary_label, broad_label, confidence, status, risk_flags. Detailed evidence remains stored interpretation table object@misc. label_candidates Optional vector candidate cell-type labels broad lineages treated annotation priors sorted enriched datasets. injected prompt constraints, model may still return broader label evidence weak. label_style Naming style used normalize returned cell-type labels. One "title", "snake", "asis". return_prompt TRUE, return prompt bundle without calling provider. store_name Name used object@misc$interpretation_results. return_object TRUE, return updated Seurat object. show_progress Logical; TRUE, emit step-wise progress logs , cli available, console progress bar waiting LLM response. cluster_col Deprecated alias cluster_by. ... Additional arguments forwarded provider.

title: Interpret cluster markers for cell-type annotation — sn_interpret_annotation
code: if (requireNamespace("Seurat", quietly = TRUE)) {   counts <- matrix(rpois(20 * 24, lambda = 1), nrow = 20, ncol = 24)   rownames(counts) <- c(     paste0("GENE", 1:14),     "CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC", "MS4A1", "CD79A", "HLA-DRA"   )   colnames(counts) <- paste0("cell", 1:24)   counts[c("CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC"), 1:12] <-     counts[c("CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC"), 1:12] + 20   counts[c("MS4A1", "CD79A", "HLA-DRA"), 13:24] <-     counts[c("MS4A1", "CD79A", "HLA-DRA"), 13:24] + 20   obj <- sn_initialize_seurat_object(counts, species = "human")   obj$cell_type <- rep(c("Tcell", "Bcell"), each = 12)   Seurat::Idents(obj) <- obj$cell_type   obj <- Seurat::NormalizeData(obj, verbose = FALSE)   obj <- sn_find_de(obj, analysis = "markers", group_by = "cell_type",     layer = "data", min_pct = 0, logfc_threshold = 0,     store_name = "celltype_markers", return_object = TRUE, verbose = FALSE   )   prompt <- sn_interpret_annotation(     obj,     de_name = "celltype_markers",     cluster_col = "cell_type",     return_prompt = TRUE   )   prompt$task } #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:14:49] Initializing Seurat object for project: Shennong. #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:14:49] Running QC metrics for human. #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:14:49] Seurat object initialization complete. #> Warning: `cluster_col` is deprecated; use `cluster_by` instead. #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:14:49] [sn_interpret_annotation] Starting interpretation workflow. #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:14:49] [sn_interpret_annotation] Step 1/5: Preparing annotation evidence (elapsed 0.0s). #> As of Seurat v5, we recommend using AggregateExpression to perform pseudo-bulk analysis. #> This message is displayed once per session. #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:14:49] [sn_interpret_annotation] Step 2/5: Building annotation prompt (elapsed 0.1s). #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:14:49] [sn_interpret_annotation] Prompt prepared (total elapsed 0.1s). #> [1] "annotation"

code: if (requireNamespace("Seurat", quietly = TRUE)) {   counts <- matrix(rpois(20 * 24, lambda = 1), nrow = 20, ncol = 24)   rownames(counts) <- c(     paste0("GENE", 1:14),     "CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC", "MS4A1", "CD79A", "HLA-DRA"   )   colnames(counts) <- paste0("cell", 1:24)   counts[c("CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC"), 1:12] <-     counts[c("CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC"), 1:12] + 20   counts[c("MS4A1", "CD79A", "HLA-DRA"), 13:24] <-     counts[c("MS4A1", "CD79A", "HLA-DRA"), 13:24] + 20   obj <- sn_initialize_seurat_object(counts, species = "human")   obj$cell_type <- rep(c("Tcell", "Bcell"), each = 12)   Seurat::Idents(obj) <- obj$cell_type   obj <- Seurat::NormalizeData(obj, verbose = FALSE)   obj <- sn_find_de(obj, analysis = "markers", group_by = "cell_type",     layer = "data", min_pct = 0, logfc_threshold = 0,     store_name = "celltype_markers", return_object = TRUE, verbose = FALSE   )   prompt <- sn_interpret_de(obj, de_name = "celltype_markers", return_prompt = TRUE)   prompt$task } #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:14:49] Initializing Seurat object for project: Shennong. #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:14:49] Running QC metrics for human. #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:14:50] Seurat object initialization complete. #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:14:50] [sn_interpret_de] Starting interpretation workflow. #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:14:50] [sn_interpret_de] Step 1/4: Preparing DE evidence (elapsed 0.0s). #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:14:50] [sn_interpret_de] Step 2/4: Building interpretation prompt (elapsed 0.0s). #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:14:50] [sn_interpret_de] Prompt prepared (total elapsed 0.0s). #> [1] "de"

what: Load example datasets from Zenodo — sn_load_data
title: Load example datasets from Zenodo — sn_load_data
text: function downloads (already cached) loads processed single-cell RNA-seq example datasets Zenodo-backed registry. current registry contains PBMC example datasets (pbmc1k, pbmc3k, pbmc4k, pbmc8k), interface intentionally generalized additional example datasets can added later without introducing another top-level loader function.

title: Load example datasets from Zenodo — sn_load_data
code: sn_load_data(   dataset = "pbmc3k",   matrix_type = c("filtered", "raw"),   save_dir = "~/.shennong/data",   return_object = TRUE,   species = NULL,   token = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Load example datasets from Zenodo — sn_load_data
text: dataset Character vector. example dataset(s) load. Currently one "pbmc1k", "pbmc3k", "pbmc4k", "pbmc8k". Default: "pbmc3k". matrix_type Character scalar. matrix type load. One : "filtered": filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5 (Cell Ranger filtered barcodes). "raw": raw_feature_bc_matrix.h5 (unfiltered barcodes; useful ambient RNA correction tools SoupX). Default: "filtered". save_dir Character scalar. Local cache directory downloaded Zenodo files stored. directory created exist. Default: "~/.shennong/data". return_object Logical. TRUE (default), function returns -memory object. FALSE, function ensures file downloaded locally returns (invisibly) local file path. species Character scalar. Species label passed sn_initialize_seurat_object() constructing Seurat objects (.e. matrix_type == "filtered"). Ignored matrix_type == "raw". NULL, use dataset default example-data registry. loading multiple datasets, supply either one species label datasets one label per dataset. token Optional Zenodo access token. Public example datasets require token. Supply restricted/private records. overwrite Logical. TRUE, re-download cached files. quiet Logical. TRUE, suppress Zenodo download progress messages.

title: Load example datasets from Zenodo — sn_load_data
text: One : matrix_type == "filtered" return_object = TRUE: Seurat object. Multiple datasets returned one merged Seurat object. matrix_type == "raw" return_object = TRUE: sparse count matrix (e.g. dgCMatrix) , multiple datasets, named list sparse count matrices. return_object = FALSE: local file path cached .h5 file, named character vector paths multiple datasets, returned invisibly.

title: Load example datasets from Zenodo — sn_load_data
text: Instead storing serialized R objects, function caches original Zenodo .h5 files locally reproducibility version consistency. requested, dynamically constructs returns either: Seurat object (filtered data), sparse count matrix (raw data). Users can also choose download/cache data without loading memory. datasets re-aligned using Cell Ranger v9.0.1 custom reference based GENCODE v48 (GRCh38.p14). Caching strategy function caches original files Zenodo first use: example, loading pbmc3k may see: return_object = TRUE, cached .h5 file read fly: matrix_type == "filtered": Seurat object constructed via sn_initialize_seurat_object(). multiple datasets requested, per-dataset Seurat objects merged source recorded sample metadata column. matrix_type == "raw": function returns sparse count matrix (typically dgCMatrix), suitable ambient RNA estimation / SoupX workflows. multiple raw datasets requested, matrices returned named list. return_object = FALSE, object constructed; file ensured exist locally. Multiple datasets return named character vector cached paths.
code: {dataset}_{matrix_type}_feature_bc_matrix.h5 ~/.shennong/data/ |- pbmc1k_filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5 |- pbmc1k_raw_feature_bc_matrix.h5 |- pbmc3k_filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5 |- pbmc3k_raw_feature_bc_matrix.h5 |- pbmc4k_filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5 |- pbmc4k_raw_feature_bc_matrix.h5 |- pbmc8k_filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5 \- pbmc8k_raw_feature_bc_matrix.h5

title: Run clustering for a single dataset or batch integration workflow — sn_run_cluster
text: object Seurat object. batch column name object@meta.data specifying batch labels used integration. NULL, integration performed. normalization_method One "seurat", "scran", "sctransform". "seurat" "scran" workflows can followed supported integration_method batch supplied. SCTransform workflow can currently combined integration_method = "harmony" supplying batch. integration_method Batch-integration backend used batch supplied. Supported values "harmony", "coralysis", "seurat_cca", "seurat_rpca", "scvi", "scanvi". "harmony" preserves historical Shennong behavior. "coralysis" runs Coralysis multi-level integration selected log-normalized feature set stores integrated embedding "coralysis" reduction. "scvi" "scanvi" export selected count matrix pixi-managed scverse environment ~/.shennong/pixi/, run Python backend, import latent representation Seurat reduction. integration_control Optional named list backend-specific parameters. "coralysis", use icp_args Coralysis::RunParallelDivisiveICP() arguments, pca_args Coralysis::RunPCA() arguments, store_sce = TRUE keep Coralysis SingleCellExperiment object@misc$coralysis. "seurat_cca" "seurat_rpca", values forwarded Seurat::IntegrateLayers(). "scvi" "scanvi", common fields include runtime_dir, pixi_project, pixi_home, run_dir, pixi, manifest_path, install_pixi, accelerator, cuda_version, mirror, n_latent, max_epochs, model_args, train_args, write_h5ad; "scanvi" additionally requires label_by accepts unlabeled_category. Use sn_pixi_paths() inspect generated directory layout, sn_pixi_config_path() inspect bundled inst/pixi/ config, sn_ensure_pixi() preinstall pixi, sn_configure_pixi_mirror() set Shennong-level mirrors. nfeatures Number variable features select. hvg_features Optional character vector user-supplied features force feature set used scaling/PCA. features merged internally selected HVGs rare-aware features validating present object. vars_to_regress Covariates regress ScaleData. resolution Resolution parameter FindClusters. cluster_algorithm Community-detection algorithm passed Seurat::FindClusters(). Supported names "louvain" (Seurat algorithm 1), "louvain_multilevel" (algorithm 2), "slm" (algorithm 3), "leiden" (algorithm 4). Numeric values 1 4 also accepted. cluster_name Optional metadata column name cluster labels. Defaults Seurat's "seurat_clusters" behavior. cluster_n_start, cluster_n_iter Number starts iterations passed Seurat::FindClusters(). cluster_random_seed Random seed passed Seurat::FindClusters(). cluster_group_singletons Whether Seurat::FindClusters() group singletons nearest cluster. leiden_method Leiden implementation passed Seurat::FindClusters() cluster_algorithm = "leiden". leiden_objective_function Leiden objective function passed Seurat::FindClusters(). cluster_control Optional named list additional Seurat::FindClusters() arguments. Values override Shennong's generated defaults. reuse Logical; TRUE, reuse previously recorded sn_run_cluster() stages stored input signatures still match current call. lets resolution-changes start clustering, integration-method changes start integration, HVG changes start feature selection instead rerunning earlier steps. rerun_from Optional stage name forcing recomputation stage onward still allowing earlier matching stages reused. Supported values "normalize", "cell_cycle", "hvg", "pca", "integration", "neighbors", "clusters", "umap". auto_install Logical; TRUE, install missing optional clustering dependencies leidenbase relevant stage. install_repos CRAN-like repositories used auto_install installs CRAN packages. install_ask Passed BiocManager::install() auto_install installs Bioconductor packages sn_install_dependencies(). hvg_group_by Optional metadata column used compute highly variable genes within groups merging ranking . NULL batch supplied, Shennong reuses batch default. Use NULL batch = NULL compute HVGs full object. rare_feature_method Optional rare-cell-aware feature methods appended base HVG set PCA/clustering. Supported values "none", "gini", "local_markers". rare_feature_group_by Optional metadata column used define groups "local_markers". NULL, Shennong builds temporary coarse clustering base HVGs. rare_feature_n Number rare-aware features add per selected method. example, c("gini", "local_markers") rare_feature_n = 50 can contribute 100 rare-aware features de-duplication. rare_feature_control Named list advanced rare-feature thresholds. Supported fields group_max_fraction, group_max_cells, gene_max_fraction, min_cells. rare_group_max_fraction, rare_group_max_cells, rare_gene_max_fraction Deprecated rare-feature thresholds. Use rare_feature_control instead. block_genes Either character vector predefined bundled signature categories (example c("ribo","mito")) custom vector gene symbols exclude HVGs. theta theta parameter harmony::RunHarmony, controlling batch diversity preservation vs. correction. title: Transfer labels from a Seurat reference to a query object — sn_transfer_labels
text: sn_transfer_labels() Shennong wrapper reference mapping. keeps common path compact: transfer one metadata label, add predicted label_by confidence score back query, store small provenance record query@misc$label_transfer. default method = "seurat" wraps Seurat's FindTransferAnchors() TransferData() workflow. method = "coralysis" projects query onto Coralysis-trained reference Coralysis::ReferenceMapping(). method = "scanvi" method = "scarches" use managed scVI-family pixi backend train semi-supervised scANVI model reference labels query cells marked unlabeled, import predicted query labels.

title: Transfer labels from a Seurat reference to a query object — sn_transfer_labels
text: object Seurat query object annotate. argument comes first function can used pipes. reference labeled Seurat reference object. label_by Metadata column reference transfer. method Label-transfer backend. "seurat" uses Seurat anchors; "coralysis" uses Coralysis::ReferenceMapping() requires Coralysis-trained reference stored reference@misc$coralysis; "scanvi" "scarches" use scVI-family pixi backend. query Deprecated alias object; retained compatibility old reference-first call style. prediction_prefix Prefix metadata columns added query. Defaults paste0(label_col, "_transfer"). normalization_method Normalization method passed Seurat::FindTransferAnchors(). reference_assay, query_assay Assays passed Seurat::FindTransferAnchors() Seurat::TransferData(). method = "coralysis", query_assay controls assay converted query logcounts; reference assay ignored stored Coralysis reference available. reference_layer, query_layer Layers used log-normalized expression method = "coralysis". query defaults Seurat "data" layer normalized first layer absent. reduction Dimensional reduction strategy passed Seurat::FindTransferAnchors(). reference_reduction Optional reference reduction passed Seurat::FindTransferAnchors(). features Optional features used find transfer anchors. dims Dimensions used anchor scoring label_by transfer. npcs Number PCs used Seurat::FindTransferAnchors(). k_anchor, k_filter, k_score, k_weight Seurat anchor/weighting parameters. store_prediction_scores TRUE, also store per-label prediction scores query metadata columns. return_anchors TRUE, return list containing annotated query backend artifacts. Coralysis, artifact mapped SingleCellExperiment. transfer_control Optional backend-specific list. method = "coralysis", values forwarded Coralysis::ReferenceMapping(). method = "scanvi" "scarches", common values include batch_by, runtime_dir, pixi_project, max_epochs, scanvi_max_epochs, accelerator, mirror, install_pixi. verbose Whether print Seurat progress messages. label_col Deprecated alias label_by. ... Defaults "public". keywords Optional subject keywords. manifest TRUE, upload shennong_zenodo_manifest.json file checksums provenance. manifest_dir Directory used write temporary manifest file. reserve_doi Whether reserve DOI new drafts. dry_run TRUE, build return planned record manifest without contacting Zenodo. logger Logger level passed zen4R::ZenodoManager$new().

code: if (requireNamespace("Seurat", quietly = TRUE)) {   counts <- matrix(rpois(10 * 24, lambda = 1), nrow = 10, ncol = 24)   rownames(counts) <- c(     "CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC", "LTB", "MS4A1",     "CD79A", "HLA-DRA", "LYZ", "ACTB", "MALAT1"   )   colnames(counts) <- paste0("cell", 1:24)   obj <- sn_initialize_seurat_object(counts, species = "human")   obj$cell_type <- rep(c("Tcell", "Bcell"), each = 12)   Seurat::Idents(obj) <- obj$cell_type   obj <- Seurat::NormalizeData(obj, verbose = FALSE)   obj <- sn_find_de(obj, analysis = "markers", group_by = "cell_type",     layer = "data", min_pct = 0, logfc_threshold = 0,     store_name = "celltype_markers", return_object = TRUE, verbose = FALSE   )   obj <- sn_store_enrichment(     obj,     tibble::tibble(ID = "GO:0001", Description = "immune response", NES = 2, p.adjust = 0.01),     store_name = "demo_gsea"   )   prompt <- sn_write_results(     obj,     cluster_de_name = "celltype_markers",     enrichment_name = "demo_gsea",     cluster_col = "cell_type",     return_prompt = TRUE   )   prompt$task } #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:15:21] Initializing Seurat object for project: Shennong. #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:15:21] Running QC metrics for human. #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:15:21] Seurat object initialization complete. #> Warning: No DE genes identified #> Warning: `cluster_col` is deprecated; use `cluster_by` instead. #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:15:22] [sn_write_results] Starting interpretation workflow. #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:15:22] [sn_write_results] Step 1/4: Preparing results evidence (elapsed 0.0s). #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:15:22] [sn_write_results] Step 2/4: Building writing prompt (elapsed 0.1s). #> INFO [2026-05-05 21:15:22] [sn_write_results] Prompt prepared (total elapsed 0.1s). #> [1] "results"

title: Version 0.1.2
text: Bundled human mouse GENCODE gene-annotation data added gene-level filtering workflows. snapshot now also stores genome-context fields sequence name, source, coordinates, strand, gene status/source, annotation level addition gene identifiers gene types. Small built-PBMC example assets added pbmc_small pbmc_small_raw, sampled packaged pbmc1k / pbmc3k references check-safe examples README workflows. Integration cluster-diagnostics metrics expanded sn_calculate_silhouette(), sn_calculate_graph_connectivity(), sn_calculate_pcr_batch(), sn_calculate_clustering_agreement(), sn_calculate_isolated_label_score(), sn_calculate_cluster_entropy(), sn_calculate_cluster_purity(), sn_identify_challenging_groups(), aggregate sn_assess_integration() wrapper. Rare-cell-aware clustering support added sn_detect_rare_cells() new sn_run_cluster() parameters can append rare-aware features Gini-selected genes, local HVGs, local markers, optional CIARA-derived features PCA Harmony. Bulk RNA-seq deconvolution support added sn_deconvolve_bulk(), sn_store_deconvolution(), sn_get_deconvolution_result(), covering local BayesPrism runs plus local CIBERSORTx container workflows result import. pkgdown documentation now reorganized around workflow stages rather source files alone. New end--end articles cover preprocessing QC, clustering integration, metrics diagnostics, annotation pathways, composition analysis, interpretation/reporting. sn_find_de() now supports pseudobulk differential expression limma addition DESeq2 edgeR. sn_find_de() now supports marker discovery COSGR optional GitHub package installed. sn_initialize_project() now scaffolds user analysis repositories shipped inst/codex/project-template/ assets, creating governed project layout AGENTS.md, memory/, docs/standards/, skills/, config/, data/, scripts/, notebooks/, runs/, results/. sn_initialize_project() now convenience wrapper packaged project-template initializer. sn_get_codex_skill_path() now exposes packaged Codex asset paths Codex root, package-usage skills, project template, project-template skills. sn_install_codex_skill() now installs package-usage skills, project governance skills, packaged Codex asset layout. Signature catalog helpers added: sn_list_signatures(), sn_add_signature(), sn_update_signature(), sn_delete_signature(). Stored-result discovery retrieval helpers added: sn_list_results(), sn_get_de_result(), sn_get_enrichment_result(), sn_get_interpretation_result(). interpretation layer now supports user-supplied background context dual output styles either model-facing prompt bundles human-readable summaries.

title: Version 0.1.2
text: Internal sparse-matrix workflows optimized better runtime lower peak memory use. Pseudobulk DE aggregation now groups columns without materializing dense matrices, split Seurat assay layers combined sparse triplet assembly instead repeated indexed writes, exact kNN fallbacks now use blockwise distance evaluation instead constructing full cell--cell distance matrix, gene-symbol standardization now reuses bundled annotation data plus grouped row aggregation instead external CSV reads per-column duplicate collapsing. Developer-facing informational notifications now routed internal package logging helpers instead mix ad hoc logger, message(), cli progress calls. Progress wording now consistent across initialization, clustering, enrichment, preprocessing workflows, real warning() stop() conditions retain existing semantics. sn_enrich() now uses single x input automatic dispatch across gene vectors, ranked named vectors, data frames, Seurat-stored DE results. gene_clusters formula now drives grouped ORA (gene ~ cluster) ranked GSEA (gene ~ log2fc) workflows, multi-database requests database = c("H", "GOBP", "KEGG", "C2:CP:REACTOME") supported one call. sn_enrich() now aligns MSigDB arguments msigdbr supporting collection / subcollection, still accepting collection strings database H C2:CP:REACTOME. sn_enrich() now filters returned enrichment tables raw p-value rather adjusted p-value writes one result file per requested database stable prefix / outdir naming. Dataset documentation now consolidated single R/data.R source, user-facing examples shifting simulated matrices network-backed PBMC downloads built-small PBMC package data. sn_find_de() now uses single method argument instead separate test_use pseudobulk_method parameters. sn_find_de() now uses layer consistently Seurat v5 workflows longer exposes legacy slot argument. sn_filter_genes() now supports annotation-aware filtering gene_class exact gene_type values backed bundled GENCODE snapshot human mouse. Signature registry maintenance now delegates add/update/delete operations upstream SignatuR package API instead maintaining parallel custom tree-editing implementation inside Shennong. Harmony-backed integration now targets immunogenomics/harmony harmony2 developer branch package metadata CI instead CRAN release line. Integration metrics now prefer stored Seurat neighbor graphs available otherwise fall back Annoy-based approximate kNN exact distance search, keeping default assessment path fast enough routine use. sn_get_signatures() now reads package-owned signature snapshot built full SignatuR tree development, runtime signature retrieval stable, tree-structured, longer depends installed SignatuR package version. Signature build assets now center data/shennong_signature_catalog.rda, rebuilt directly upstream SignatuR dataset development, replacing opaque R/sysdata.rda storage used earlier snapshot prototype. sn_enrich() now stores enrichment results object@misc$enrichment_results[[store_name]] Seurat object supplied, aligning enrichment existing stored DE workflow. pkgdown articles, shipped Codex skill references, NEWS.md now treated required deliverables user-facing workflow change.
